title: The Putt-Putt Road Trip Adventure
summary: Mickey, Minnie, and Mole finish a picnic in Mole’s flower-filled yard and decide to go exploring in the deep forest. They pile into Mole’s small, noisy brown car and set off on a road trip. After realizing they forgot snacks, they stop at Mischief Mouse’s store to stock up on cheese and ants. Their journey is interrupted by a heavy traffic jam, but they pass the time by visiting Red Mouse's unique toy store, which is built entirely of wooden blocks. After purchasing magnetiles and a dress-up doll, they return to their car, wait for the green light, and zoom off once the road is clear.

highlightQuote.text: Well you could just stop at a toy store, you could just stop at a toy store and get some toys to play with while you're waiting.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Finn again!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oops, Finn-cat. You have to put him downstairs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, I'll just bring him down really quick and then I'll be back up in a minute.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, what are you doing down there?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm just putting Finn-cat down there.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What? What? What? What were you doing? What were you doing downstairs?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I was just closing up the gate so that Finn-cat couldn't come back up. It just took me a minute. All right. All right, I've got the blue timer, so we know how long our story's going to be.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I want a story about Mickey, Minnie, Mole. Mickey, Minnie, Mole. Mickey, Minnie, Mole. Mickey and Minnie and Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mickey and Minnie and Mole. And should we tell a story about Mickey and Minnie and Mole staying home or going on an adventure or visiting their friends?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Having a nice time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Having a nice time? But were they at home or did they go out somewhere?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were out somewhere.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Mickey and Minnie and Mole having a nice time out somewhere. Let's see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some others do, some others don't. Now you can tell a story. Dad, some others can and some others can't.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They can't what? I liked your little song about that. Well, one day, Mickey and Minnie and Mole were having a little picnic in Mole's front yard. And Mole had a very nice front yard because he had very lush green grass with lots of dandelions and other flowers. And they were sitting on the blanket and they had just finished up their nice little lunch when they decided to go exploring.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, there were parts of the forest they'd never been to, so they thought they would take a path and see where it led them. Because there were lots of paths through the forest. And did they decide to go walking on their feet or did they take bicycles or scooters or go in some other way?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They went in a very long... they went in a car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they went in a car so they could go really far. And it just so happened that Mole didn't drive a lot, but he did have a little car. It was a little old car and it was dark brown and it was kind of noisy and very small. But everybody crowded in, they squeezed in together, Mole at the driver's... in the driver's seat driving the car and Mickey and Minnie next to him. And he started the car, vroom, putt-putt-putt-putt-putt, because this car made a little bit of a putt-putt sound. And he said, 'Okay everyone, let's go. We're just going to find out where we go on the way.' And so they went vroom, vroom, vroom, putt-putt-putt-putt-putt onto the road. And as they drove along on the road, right away they realized something they had forgotten to bring with them. You know what they forgot to bring?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Provisions.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Exactly, provisions. They didn't bring any snacks. First rule of going on a road trip, you need snacks for the car. And so Mole said, 'Oh, I see a little place to pull over.' It happened to be a little store that was run by a friend of his. Who was the friend that owned the store?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mischief Mousey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Of course, it was Mischief Mouse's store. And what did they sell at Mischief Mouse's store?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cheese!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cheese! And that's one of a mouse's favorite snacks, of course. But Moles don't necessarily like cheese.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, so there's ants at the store too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah! So they parked their car in the lot and they went in the door which made a little ringing sound because the door rang a bell when they went in. Bading! And Mischief Mouse said, 'Oh hey friends, what are you up to today?' And they said, 'We're going on a road trip but we need some provisions.' And so they shopped around a little bit, bought some nice cheese snacks and some ants to snack on in the car and they got back in the car and they went back on their way. And before long they were going putt-putt-putt-putt-putt along the road once again. And then they got stuck in a traffic jam because there were lots of other cars on the road.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well but you could keep going when there's a green light.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's true. Sometimes you have to stop in traffic and there's a red light. But then if you wait a little bit it turns green. But they were waiting and waiting and there was so much traffic that they didn't know what to do because the car couldn't go.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well you could just stop at a toy store, you could just stop at a toy store and get some toys to play with while you're waiting.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mole said, 'Hey, that's a good idea! And there is a toy store just over there.' And so they pulled off the road and drove over to the little toy store. And whose toy store was it? Was it...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Red Mouse's store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, Red Mouse's toy store. A wonderful but very small toy store. And the toy store actually wasn't made of a building. It was made of something else. What was it? What was it made of? Was the toy store inside of a cardboard box or was it under a table or was it inside a bush or was it under the ground? Where was Red Mouse's toy store? Or was it made of blocks?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was made of blocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, Red Mouse's toy store happened to be made of blocks and it was a beautiful elaborately decorated building made of wooden blocks. And inside were all kinds of little toys if you were small enough to fit through the entrance. But fortunately Mickey and Minnie and Mole were all pretty small creatures too so they could go in and shop a little bit. And what did they decide to buy? I wonder what. Did they buy some magnetiles?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, they bought some magnetiles and one doll.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And one doll. Oh, what kind of a doll was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a dress-up doll.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds like fun! So now they had some nice toys to bring with them back in the car so that while they waited for the traffic to clear and the light to turn green they'd have plenty to do while they waited. And so they brought their toys and got back in the car and went putt-putt-putt-putt-putt and before long they were out of the traffic and zooming along the road. And I think that's going to be the end of our story for tonight because we're out of time.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So now turn on the lamp. It's too dark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Oh, and the blue is flashing.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A resourceful, perpetually humming mole with an impossibly long name, this polite and intrepid mariner serves as a bridge between worlds, translating Frasgle’s unique tongue and guiding the way to the hidden Underground Library. Whether managing rocket controls with wide-eyed curiosity, unraveling treehouse mysteries, or sharing the magic of special Legos, he encourages his companions to embrace the unknown between tending his dandelion yard and baking fragrant banana bread. A lover of both wriggly worms and sweet blueberries, he sings whimsical songs of carnabee sharks and drives a noisy dark brown car, remaining firmly hesitant about boarding giant robots even as he coaxes his friends toward bold new adventures.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Red Mouse
characters.description: The owner of a toy store made entirely out of wooden blocks.

characters.name: Finn-cat
characters.description: The family cat who needs to be moved downstairs before the story starts.

places.name: Mole's Front Yard
places.description: A pleasant yard with lush green grass, dandelions, and flowers.

places.name: The Forest
places.description: Cloaking the surface of the green planet in a vast mantle of dense greenery, this whispering woodland serves as the lush setting of the 'Forest Friends' chronicle. It is a realm where creatures play eternal hide-and-seek between Hattie’s cozy clinic and the ancestral cradle of Finn’s companions. Along magical trails where Hattie and the Pakruder Worm harvest hat-making ingredients, winding paths veil secret gopher tunnels and hidden mole cottages, forming the essential route toward the wizard's castle. While its sun-drenched threshold remains a sanctuary for the Rainbow Giraffe and Mama Bird, the deeper, darker thickets harbor a somber mystery; it was within these sprawling, uncharted depths—the same eerie expanse where the sick creature was first spotted and Mossy was discovered—that Mole recently vanished into the shadows.

places.name: Mischief Mouse's Store
places.description: Nestled along the thoroughfare, this traveler’s waypoint offers a peculiar bounty: pungent wheels of cheese and jars of dried ants sit alongside a neighboring cache of practical gear. Here, a wanderer can stock their pack with provisions before stepping next door to weather the elements with sturdy rain jackets, umbrellas, and polished galoshes.

places.name: Red Mouse's Toy Store
places.description: An elaborately decorated small building constructed from wooden blocks.
